title: thymus serpyllum var. litoralis
intro: Thymus serpyllum var. litoralis, also known as sea thyme or maritime thyme, is a perennial herb native to the coastal regions of Europe and North Africa, characterized by its small, pink to purple flowers and woody stems. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include its antimicrobial, antifungal, and antiseptic properties, making it effective against a range of infections and wounds. The health benefits of Thymus serpyllum var. litoralis include its ability to boost the immune system, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antioxidant properties, which can help to protect against oxidative stress and cell damage. The plant can be prepared as a herbal tea, tincture, or infused oil, allowing for its medicinal properties to be harnessed in a variety of forms.
